$comment: Commercial offers for this product. v2.0 introduces offer_groups[] with shared{} inheritance. One entry per purchasable option. Facts only — what is available, at what price, under what conditions. Pre-cart scope only (post-cart = future funnel.json).

_changelog.2.0: MAJOR. Hard-break from flat offers[] (v1.x) to offer_groups[] + shared{} + offers[]. Merge rules: objects deep-merge, arrays replace, scalars override explicitly. Merge MUST be resolved BEFORE JSON Schema validation. Added meta.scope='pre_cart' const to codify offer/funnel frontier. Type enum reduced 12→10 (removed hardware_bundle → bundle+tags[hardware]+requires_offer_id; removed personalized → any type + personalization{} block pointer). subscription.intro_discount scalar → subscription.intro_phases[] array. Added membership{} block distinct from subscription (Amazon Prime / Thrive Market pattern). Removed zombie fields: commitment_duration, recurring_discount, skip_window, qty_per_variant, matrix_dimensions, supply_guarantee. 9th semantic check on requires_offer_id cycle detection runs post-merge in validate-all.py.
_changelog.1.8: Batch 5 stress test final flat-schema version. See _ARCHIVE for legacy.

offer_groups.name: Default group
offer_groups.description: One group per product as minimum. Split into multiple groups only when offers share distinct defaults (shipping, returns, warranty, payment_methods, active_window, product_refs). Group-of-1 is a valid pattern — use new_offer.py generator to avoid boilerplate.

shared.$comment: Shared defaults for all offers in this group. MERGE RULES: (1) nested objects (pricing, subscription, contents, etc.) merge deep — offer wins on key collision. (2) arrays (payment_methods, tags, product_refs) replace entirely when set at offer level. (3) scalars and booleans override explicitly. VALIDATION TIMING: validator MUST resolve merge (shared → offer) BEFORE running JSON Schema validation.

_notes.type: single | subscription | bundle | prepay | membership | gifting | seasonal | launch | digital | service
_notes.type.bundle_hardware: Hardware + consumable lock-in (Bruvi, Gozney, Keurig) = type 'bundle' + tags ['hardware'] + requires_offer_id pointing to the consumable offer.
_notes.type.personalized: Quiz/AI-selected dynamic offers (Care/of, Prose, Function of Beauty) = keep the base type (subscription/one_time/bundle) and add a personalization{} block with {source, source_id, dynamic_pricing, dynamic_contents}. The offers.json only holds the pointer — rules engine lives elsewhere.
_notes.product_refs: Array of {slug, quantity}. Required at offer level unless identical across group and set in shared. minItems: 1.
_notes.requires_offer_id: For hardware+refill, membership+checkout, trial+converts. Points to another offer_id in this doc or a cross-product offer_id. Cycle detection runs in validate-all.py 9th semantic check.
_notes.subscription.intro_phases: Array of {from_cycle, to_cycle, price|discount_type+value, label}. Replaces the v1.8 scalar intro_discount. Cycle null = phase open until churn. Last open phase = source of truth for steady-state pricing.
_notes.membership: Distinct from subscription. Fields: billing_period, perks[], catalog_discount{applies_to, tag_filter}, includes_products[], physical_shipment. Use when the offer is access+perks, not delivery of a single recurring product.
_notes.meta.scope: Const 'pre_cart'. Codifies that offers.json only describes merchandising before add-to-cart. Post-cart (upsell, bump, thank-you page) = future funnel.json.
_notes.migration_from_v1.8: Run research/migrate_offers_v1_to_v2.py <path> --write. Group-of-1 wrapper auto-generated. Operator can regroup post-migration if multiple offers share distinct defaults.
